sourceMovie.title: Heart Eyes
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Heart Eyes works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 10 Things I Hate About You
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Heart Eyes's comedy stayed with you, 10 Things I Hate About You (1999) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The romance through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: The Apartment
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Heart Eyes excels in comedy, The Apartment (1960) doubles down on romance to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: About Fate
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: About Fate (2022) is the natural next step after Heart Eyes: same comedy DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and romance elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Prizzi's Honor
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Heart Eyes who respond to its comedy will find Prizzi's Honor (1985) immediately familiar. Both films share a romance sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Singles
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Heart Eyes fans keep landing on Singles (1992) — the comedy is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: The Wrong Paris
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Wrong Paris draws from the same comedy well as Heart Eyes, channeling romance in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2025 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Scream
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Scream (1996) laid groundwork for the tone and themes that Heart Eyes later expanded. If Heart Eyes resonated for you, this predecessor shows where that emotional DNA began.

recommendations.title: Scary Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The comedy appeal of Heart Eyes — that funny edge, the craft — translates cleanly into Scary Movie (2000). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Scream 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Scream 2 (1997) clearly understood what makes horror work — the same quality that keeps Heart Eyes rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Thanksgiving
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Thanksgiving (2023) is the kind of film Heart Eyes fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The horror overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

faq.question: What should I watch after Heart Eyes?
faq.answer: Try 10 Things I Hate About You, The Apartment, About Fate—each shares DNA with Heart Eyes while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Heart Eyes?
faq.answer: Heart Eyes (2025) is best described as Horror, Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Heart Eyes worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
